title: 10 best herbal teas for eye swelling
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye swelling are infusions made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce eye puffiness and discomfort. Chamomile, for instance, contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that can alleviate eye swelling and irritation. Similarly, calendula tea has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye inflammation and promote healing. By drinking these herbal teas or using them as compresses, individuals can experience relief from eye swelling, leading to improved vision and overall well-being. For example, a person who spends long hours in front of a computer may find relief from eye strain and puffiness by drinking chamomile tea or applying calendula tea compresses.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with eye swelling because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and redness. The tea's active ingredients, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup around the eyes, alleviating swelling and discomfort. Additionally, eyebright's soothing properties help calm irritated tissues and promote healthy eye function, making it a natural remedy for relieving puffy, tired-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in eyebright tea, such as vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and swelling.
Soothing mucilages: Eyebright tea contains soothing mucilages like glycosides, which help calm irritated eyes and reduce swelling by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the eyebright plant have astringent properties that help to reduce excess fluids and ease puffiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried eyebright herb euphrasia officinalis.
Bring fresh filtered water to a boil in a kettle or pot for 2-3 minutes.
Remove the boiling water from heat and pour it over the measured eyebright herb.
Strain the tea leaves using a fine-mesh strainer or piece of cheesecloth into another cup.
Enjoy your warm or cooled eyebright tea immediately, or store it in refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with eye swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves contain compounds that have been shown to reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes, making it an effective remedy for alleviating eye strain and fatigue. When brewed as a tea, these compounds are released into the liquid, allowing them to be absorbed through the skin around the eyes, providing relief from swelling and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea helps reduce eye swelling by reducing inflammation, which is often caused by allergies or injuries to the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in plantain tea help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling and puffiness, promoting overall eye health.
Astringent properties: Plantain tea's astringent properties help reduce fluid retention and shrink blood vessels, which can alleviate eye swelling and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes.
Bark: Containing astringent properties, bark helps constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves and flowers from your garden or store.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine one teaspoon of dried plantain herb or two teaspoons of fresh plantain leaves.
Steep the plantain mixture in boiling water for 5-10 minutes allowing flavors release slowly.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with eye swelling because it contains a unique compound called calendula, which has anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce puffiness and redness. Calendula also has antioxidant qualities that help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By drinking pot marigold tea, you can reap the benefits of these properties to soothe and calm irritated or swollen eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids and carotenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by decreasing inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and discoloration.

Vasodilatory properties: Pot marigold tea has been shown to possess vasodilatory effects, which help dilate blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation in the eyes, thereby alleviating puffiness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes.
Flowers: The flowers have antiseptic properties that prevent infections and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried calendula petals or two teaspoons of fresh petals per eight ounce cup of boiling water.
Steep the calendula petals in boiling water for five to seven minutes allowing the flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then sweeten with honey if desired and enjoy immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The antioxidants present in the tea help reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling. Additionally, chamomile's natural astringent properties help to tighten and firm the skin around the eyes, further reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffy bags.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German Chamomile contains apigenin, luteolin, and other anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from conditions such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate tissues of the eye, reducing the risk of chronic eye diseases like cataracts and macular degeneration.

Soften and protect mucous membranes: Chamomile's soothing and protective effects on mucous membranes in the eyes help reduce redness, itching, and irritation, allowing for quicker recovery from conditions such as pink eye or dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, flowers help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Roots: With a calming effect on the mucous membranes, roots contribute to reducing puffiness and redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried german chamomile flowers into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of filtered water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the chamomile flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor intensity.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug or teapot.
Add honey to taste if desired and serve hot with lemon slices optional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with eye swelling because they contain high levels of antioxidants, particularly flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes. These compounds also have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing puffiness and redness. Regular consumption of herbal white tea may also help to promote overall eye health and reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like catechins, flavonoids, and quercetin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating conditions like conjunctivitis and dry eye.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of herbal white tea helps neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, reducing the risk of eye diseases like age-related macular degeneration and cataracts.

Vasodilatory effects: Herbal white tea contains compounds like caffeine and catechins that help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce swelling and pressure in the eyes, alleviating conditions like glaucoma and conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing eye swelling and inflammation.
Bark: Containing tannins and other bioactive compounds, it helps to reduce redness and itching associated with eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and buds at dawn for optimal flavor and aroma.
Wash the leaves and buds gently in cold water to remove any impurities or dirt.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried or 3-4 teaspoons of loose leaf white tea in boiling water for 2-3 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve the tea hot or chilled, sweetened with honey or sugar if desired, within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime teas helps with eye swelling because it contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds found in herbs like calendula, chamomile, and lemon balm. These natural ingredients soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing puffiness and redness. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in the tea also help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage, further alleviating swelling. By consuming this herbal tea, individuals can experience relief from eye strain and discomfort caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Common lime tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which contributes to swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols present in lime tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling and redness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Vasodilatory properties: Lime tea's flavonoids can dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure in the eyes, which may help alleviate swelling caused by increased fluid buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and soothing irritated eyes.
Bark: The bark contains a high concentration of flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia europaea leaves in late spring or early summer when they are at their most fragrant and tender.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris before drying.
Dry the rinsed leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours to preserve their flavor and aroma.
Combine one teaspoon of dried tilia europaea leaves with one cup of boiling water and steep for five minutes to release the flavorful compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or individual cups, discarding the solids and serving hot or iced as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes. The tannins present in witch hazel tea constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid buildup and diminishing the appearance of dark circles and bags under the eyes. Additionally, witch hazel's antioxidant content combats oxidative stress, calming the delicate eye tissues and promoting a sense of relaxation.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in witch hazel tea help reduce puffiness and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention, which can alleviate swollen eyes.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in witch hazel tea, such as gallic acid, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can cause eye swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Witch hazel tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which can help reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, a hormone that promotes inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which help reduce inflammation and soothe swollen eyes.
Bark: Containing astringent compounds that constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried witch hazel leaves and twigs from a sustainable source measuring one tablespoon.
Rinse the harvested witch hazel with cool running water to remove any dirt debris or impurities steeping for three minutes.
Combine one teaspoon of dried witch hazel flowers with boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass teapot allowing it to infuse for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or piece of cheesecloth squeezing out as much liquid as possible taking about five minutes.
Serve the herbal witch hazel tea warm or at room temperature immediately after preparation with no added sweeteners or flavorings required for 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with eye swelling because it contains flavonoids, which are potent antioxidants that reduce inflammation in the eyes. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels and ease fluid buildup, thereby alleviating puffiness and reducing redness. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial compounds can help to combat infections that may be contributing to eye swelling, promoting healthy and clear vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains flavonoids and terpenes that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce eye swelling and discomfort by reducing inflammation in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in yarrow tea can neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and eye damage, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of eye swelling.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Yarrow tea contains compounds that can help constrict blood vessels, which can reduce fluid buildup and swelling in the eyes by decreasing blood flow to the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and tannins, which aid in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves and flowers from your garden or local market for best flavor quality.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried yarrow leaves and flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the measured yarrow mixture and filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized pot for five minutes.
Reduce heat to low and let steep for 10-15 minutes allowing the tea's active compounds to infuse fully.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with eye swelling because it contains potent antioxidants that reduce inflammation and relax the blood vessels around the eyes. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce puffiness and soothe red, irritated eyes, while its natural astringent properties constrict dilated blood vessels and shrink swollen tissues. This results in a calming and refreshing effect on the eyes, making it an effective herbal remedy for alleviating eye swelling and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may help increase blood flow to the eyes, reducing swelling and pressure by allowing excess fluid to be drained more effectively.
Anti-inflammatory activity: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce eye swelling by reducing blood vessel dilation.
Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to make a tea that helps reduce eye inflammation and congestion, promoting healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or purchase dried leaf extracts for use in tea preparation.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or two teaspoons of loose-leaf tea for every eight ounces of water used.
Boil filtered water in a tea kettle and pour it over the ginkgo biloba leaves or tea mixture allowing it to steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining loose leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, taking up to three cups per day as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom teas helps with eye swelling because it contains ruscogenin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body. When brewed into a tea, butcher's broom can be used as an herbal remedy to help alleviate puffy and swollen eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of the tea work to soothe and calm irritated tissues around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other underlying conditions.
remedy_properties: Ruscogenins: These saponins have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by decreasing inflammation in the surrounding tissues.
Fructans: Butcher's broom fructans have been shown to exhibit antihistaminic properties, which can help alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies or histamine release.
Fiber and saponins: The fiber and saponin content in butcher's broom tea may also help reduce fluid retention and edema in the body, including in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal teas for eye swelling because they contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes are effective in treating eye swelling due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two tablespoons of dried butcher's broom herb or one teaspoon of fresh herb.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit for five minutes.
Combine the measured herb with the heated water and allow it to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a cup or teapot and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool to drinking temperature before consumption within six hours of preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye swelling with herbal teas is to apply warm compresses soaked in cooled tea. Choose teas with anti-inflammatory properties, such as chamomile or green tea. Steep the tea in boiling water for 5-10 minutes, then let it cool. Soak a clean cloth in the tea and place it over closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties will help reduce swelling and soothe the area around the eyes. However, if swelling persists or is accompanied by other symptoms, it is important to seek medical attention.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal teas are typically related to inflammation or irritation of the eye area. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and redness around the eyes. Similarly, green tea contains bioflavonoids that can alleviate eye irritation and inflammation. Additionally, fennel tea can help soothe eye strain and fatigue, while calendula tea has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can treat eye infections and swelling. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment, as they can interact with medications or exacerbate certain medical conditions.
